repo_notes.content:
This is infra-local-shared, the shared infrastructure definition for the TCC LVC Smart Cities simulation platform. It is a Docker Compose project — not a software application — that provisions all shared local services consumed by sibling LVC microservices (event-hub, live-ms-java, virtual-ms-java, constructive-ms-python). There is no build system, test suite, or application code.

Stack: Docker Compose (orchestration), InfluxDB 2.7 (time-series database), MariaDB 11.4 (relational database), HashiCorp Vault 1.17 (secrets management, dev mode), Grafana (observability dashboards).

Architecture follows CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) with two distinct stores on each side. WRITE SIDE: InfluxDB bucket 'events' (infinite retention) for raw LVC events written by the Event Hub outbox worker; MariaDB database 'eventhub' for entity state, areas registry, and outbox table (schema managed by SQLx migrations in the event-hub repo). READ SIDE: InfluxDB bucket 'projections' (90-day retention) for aggregated time-series produced by projection workers; MariaDB database 'eventhub_read' for materialized views (area_stats, event_log) consumed by Grafana dashboards.

All services share the Docker bridge network 'shared-infra-network'. Sibling repo compose files join this network with 'external: true' to reach the shared services. The startup sequence is enforced by depends_on with healthcheck conditions: InfluxDB and MariaDB must be healthy before init containers run; init containers must complete before event-hub starts; Grafana starts after MariaDB is healthy.

Secrets strategy: external API tokens (OlhoVivo, NVIDIA NIM) are kept in .env (gitignored) and seeded into Vault KV v2 at startup by shared-vault-init. Database credentials are intentionally hardcoded in docker-compose.yml — acceptable for a local thesis environment. Vault runs in dev mode (in-memory, resets on restart).

Key directories: grafana/ (provisioning — 4 datasources, dashboard provider), mariadb/init/ (SQL init scripts for read database and schema), data/ (gitignored Docker volume mounts).

Key architectural patterns: CQRS (segregated write/read databases with user-level isolation), Infrastructure as Code (Docker Compose declarative service definitions), Healthcheck-Driven Startup (ordered dependency chain), Externalized Secrets (Vault + .env pattern).
repo_notes.author: Rafael Pereira

pages.title: Architecture Overview
pages.purpose: High-level overview of infra-local-shared's role as the infrastructure backbone of the LVC platform. Explain the CQRS architecture with segregated write and read sides across InfluxDB and MariaDB, how the shared Docker network (shared-infra-network) connects sibling microservices, and the overall data flow from event ingestion (Event Hub → InfluxDB events + MariaDB eventhub) to visualization (Grafana ← InfluxDB projections + MariaDB eventhub_read). Cover the Docker Compose philosophy (no application code, purely declarative infrastructure) and the 8 defined services.

pages.title: Services Reference
pages.purpose: Document all 8 Docker Compose services: shared-influxdb (InfluxDB 2.7, port 8086), shared-influxdb-init (bucket creation — events + projections), shared-mariadb (MariaDB 11.4, port 3306), shared-mariadb-init (read database + schema creation), shared-vault (HashiCorp Vault 1.17 dev mode, port 8200), shared-vault-init (secret seeding from .env), event-hub (Rust message broker, port 50051), shared-grafana (Grafana, port 3000). For each: image/build source, exposed ports, health check configuration, depends_on chain, volume mounts, and environment variables.

pages.title: Startup & Dependency Chain
pages.purpose: Document the ordered startup sequence enforced by depends_on with healthcheck conditions. InfluxDB and MariaDB must report healthy before their init containers run; init containers must complete (exit 0) before event-hub starts; Grafana starts after MariaDB is healthy. Explain the --force-recreate flag for re-running init containers after configuration changes, the health check implementations (pg_isready for MariaDB, influx ping for InfluxDB, curl for Vault), and troubleshooting stuck startups.

pages.title: Write Side
pages.purpose: Document the CQRS write side infrastructure. InfluxDB: bucket 'events' with infinite retention, written by the Event Hub outbox worker — stores raw LVC events as time-series measurements. MariaDB: database 'eventhub' with entity state tables, areas registry, and outbox_messages table — schema managed by SQLx migrations in the event-hub repo. Cover the eventhub MariaDB user (write-only, cannot access eventhub_read), the credentials, and how the write side guarantees atomicity via Transactional Outbox.

pages.title: Read Side
pages.purpose: Document the CQRS read side infrastructure. InfluxDB: bucket 'projections' with 90-day retention, written by projection workers — stores aggregated time-series for Grafana dashboards. MariaDB: database 'eventhub_read' with materialized views (area_stats — aggregated counters per area with composite PK; event_log — append-only denormalized event log with idempotency via UNIQUE on event_id). Cover the eventhub_read MariaDB user (read-only isolation), the SQL init scripts (mariadb/init/01_read_database.sql, 02_read_schema.sql), column types, indexes, and the utf8mb4 character set.

pages.title: Secrets Management
pages.purpose: Document the two-tier secrets strategy. External API tokens (LIVE_MS_JAVA_OLHOVIVO_TOKEN, NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY) are stored in .env (gitignored) and seeded into Vault KV v2 at startup by shared-vault-init at paths secret/live-ms-java and secret/constructive-airsim-ms. Vault runs in dev mode (root token: tcc-local-root-token, in-memory storage, resets on restart). Database credentials are hardcoded in docker-compose.yml (acceptable for local thesis use). Cover .env.example as the committed template, how to add new secrets (edit vault-init entrypoint + .env + --force-recreate), and the Vault UI at :8200.

pages.title: Grafana Dashboards & Datasources
pages.purpose: Document Grafana's auto-provisioned observability setup. Four datasources defined in grafana/provisioning/datasources/: InfluxDB Projections (default), InfluxDB Events, MariaDB Projections, MariaDB Entity State. File-based dashboard provider (grafana/provisioning/dashboards/provider.yml) with 30-second reload interval. How to add new JSON dashboards by placing files in grafana/dashboards/. Cover the admin credentials, the Grafana UI at :3000, and how dashboards consume CQRS read-side data.

pages.title: Operations Guide
pages.purpose: Practical guide for working with this infrastructure. Common Docker Compose commands: docker compose up -d (start all), docker compose down (stop all), docker compose logs -f [service] (tail logs), docker compose restart [service] (restart one), docker compose up -d --force-recreate [service] (re-run init containers). How to connect to each service: InfluxDB UI at localhost:8086, Grafana at localhost:3000 (admin/admin), Vault UI at localhost:8200, MariaDB at localhost:3306. Hardcoded local credentials for each service. Resetting state: remove data/ subdirectories and restart. Extending: adding InfluxDB buckets, Vault secrets, or Grafana datasources.
